title: Zero-scale Burgers conservative update is the identity update

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the update scale is zero, the Burgers conservative update returns the original grid value.
assumptions: u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
i is a natural-number grid index
informal.expected_conclusion: The zero-scale Burgers update is the identity update.

declarations.name: burgersConservativeUpdate
declarations.statement: def burgersConservativeUpdate (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : Real := conservativeUpdate scale u (burgersFlux u) i

declarations.name: burgersConservativeUpdate_zero_scale
declarations.statement: theorem burgersConservativeUpdate_zero_scale (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : burgersConservativeUpdate 0 u i = u i

tasks.input: Translate this Burgers conservative finite-difference theorem into Lean 4: If the update scale is zero, the Burgers conservative update returns the original grid value.
tasks.target: theorem burgersConservativeUpdate_zero_scale (u : Nat -> Real) (i : Nat) : burgersConservativeUpdate 0 u i = u i

tasks.input: Given the Burgers scaffold definitions and referenced conservation lemmas, complete the Lean 4 proof.
tasks.target: A proof by unfolding burgersConservativeUpdate and reusing conservativeUpdate_zero_scale.

tasks.input: Find the generic conservation theorem proving that a zero-scale conservative update is the identity.
